title: Milk and Dairy Products
content:
FRESH MILK

Whole Milk: At least 3.25% milkfat, 8.25% protein/lactose/minerals. Cream line forms on standing.

Homogenized: Same percentages but fat uniformly dispersed; no cream line. Finer curd, more easily digested.

Skim/Nonfat: ½% or less fat; all protein and mineral value. Fat-soluble vitamins A, D, E, K removed unless fortified.

EVAPORATED MILK

Canned whole milk with 60% moisture removed; minimum 7.5% milkfat. Reconstitute: ½ cup evaporated milk + ½ cup water = 1 cup whole milk. Invert can every few weeks during storage. Not for rennet recipes.

SWEETENED CONDENSED

50% water removed; sugar added. Minimum 8.5% milkfat. 14-ounce can = 2½ cups milk equivalent + 8 tablespoons sugar. When substituting for sweet milk, decrease sugar 2 tablespoons per ¼ cup condensed milk used.

DRY MILK SOLIDS

Air-dried to 5% moisture. Whole: minimum 26% fat. Nonfat: about 1.5% fat. Store in cool, airtight, lightproof container; refrigerate after opening.

Reconstitute: 3-4 tablespoons powder + 1 cup water = slightly more than 1 cup milk.

Cooking with dry milk: Scorch easily; use double boiler or very low heat. Mix powder with flour before adding liquid to avoid lumps. Maximum 3 tablespoons per cup liquid for sauces; ¼ cup per cup flour for baking.

SCALDING MILK

Heat until tiny bubbles form around edge (approximately 180°F) or film forms on surface. Stir film into milk before using. Rinse pan with cold water first for easier cleaning.
